records.ety: [LL. rendere to render: cf. L. redditio. See Render, and cf. Reddition.]

defs: The act of rendering; especially, the act of surrender, as of fugitives from justice, at the claim of a foreign government; also, surrender in war.
quotes: The rest of these brave men that suffered in cold blood after articles of rendition.
This rendition of the word seems also most naturally to agree with the genuine meaning of some other words in the same verse.
